objective: Resolve OpenCodex GitHub issue #86 as a verified timeout-semantics product fix. Concrete outcome: routed non-OpenAI web-search requests, especially slow local LM Studio models, must no longer misuse the global connect/header timeout as an opaque full-generation cutoff; the system must retain a finite hang guard, report the exact phase that timed out, preserve cancellation and 429 behavior, and keep eager versus in-stream response contracts coherent. Scope: inspect and, after audit, modify only the owning web-search/server/config/test/documentation surfaces such as src/web-search/loop.ts, src/web-search/index.ts, src/server/responses.ts, src/types.ts, focused tests, structure/04_transports-and-sidecars.md, and the required devlog implementation unit. Preserve all unrelated dirty-worktree changes and do not reset, checkout, stash, clean, or rewrite user work. Out of scope: GUI redesign, unrelated provider/catalog work, dependency upgrades, remote commits or pushes, GitHub issue comments, live external-provider requests, and secret-bearing configuration changes. Acceptance criteria: DONE only when a red-first regression proves the former eager timeout failure, the audited implementation distinguishes connection/header wait from slow-but-progressing or separately bounded generation behavior, first-iteration and later-iteration timeout contracts are tested, hosted sidecar non-invocation or invocation is asserted at the right phase, cancellation and 429 rotation remain bounded, error and request-log wording identifies the timed-out phase, relevant source-of-truth docs match code, targeted tests and project typecheck/build pass, and an independent sol-medium review reports no blocking findings. NOOP only if fresh evidence proves current code already meets every criterion. BLOCKED means a required local tool or repository invariant is externally unavailable after three evidence-backed attempts. UNSAFE means completion would require destructive git operations, secrets, or live provider impact. NEEDS_HUMAN means a genuinely product-defining choice cannot be resolved from current code and issue evidence. BUDGET_EXHAUSTED applies only to an explicit user budget; none is imposed here, so continue through compaction using durable artifacts. Verification evidence: red/green focused Bun tests, request-log and timeout contract tests, bun test for affected suites, project typecheck or build command from package.json, git diff inspection, source-of-truth consistency check, and independent review. Expected terminal outcome: DONE with no pending criteria. First work-phase: reproduce the old failure in a deterministic harness, map existing timeout/cancellation boundaries, and write the diff-level audited plan before implementation. Execution mode: HOTL spec-satisfaction repair loop. Tool and credential scope: local filesystem, local Bun tests, read-only git history/status, cxc state tools, and sol-medium subagents only; no remote writes or provider credentials. Write scope is limited to the files and devlog unit named above. Token, cost, and wall-clock bounds: no user-imposed cap; continue until verified DONE or an honest BLOCKED, UNSAFE, or NEEDS_HUMAN terminal condition.

workPhases.title: Separate web-search header wait from model-generation stalls

tasks.title: Capture a deterministic red regression for the eager first-iteration timeout
Implement the audited finite header and generation timeout contract
Preserve first/later iteration, cancellation, sidecar, and 429 behavior
Synchronize phase-accurate errors, source-of-truth docs, and the implementation log
Run focused and project gates plus an independent sol-medium review

criteria.scenario: The former eager first-iteration 200-second cutoff is represented by a deterministic red-first regression and no longer treats slow model generation as a header/connect failure
criteria.expectedEvidence: Focused Bun test fails on the baseline behavior, then passes after the implementation
criteria.capturedEvidence: Red: bun test tests/web-search.test.ts -t BUG-R86 exited 1 with 0 pass/3 fail, activating forced nonstream parsing, a progressing body killed by the connect deadline, and unsafe sidecar dispatch after an error. Green: the same BUG-R86 filter passes 3/3; routed iterations require stream:true, raw progress survives beyond connectTimeoutMs, and terminal error yields zero sidecar calls.

criteria.scenario: Header wait and model-generation progress have distinct finite guards, and progressing generation is not aborted by the header timeout
criteria.expectedEvidence: Unit tests for header timeout, generation stall timeout, and progress-reset behavior
criteria.capturedEvidence: Distinct-clock suites pass: clearable deadline 3/3, progress collector 20/20, timeout plan 4/4, bounded body 10/10, and runtime timeout contracts 7/7. The delayed-body regression runs beyond a 25ms header budget while non-empty bytes keep generation alive; continuous silence produces the exact routed-model inactivity timeout.

criteria.scenario: First-iteration and later-iteration failures retain their JSON versus SSE contracts and invoke or skip the hosted sidecar at the correct boundary
criteria.expectedEvidence: Focused contract tests assert status/body framing and sidecar call counts
criteria.capturedEvidence: tests/web-search-timeout-contract.test.ts passes 7/7: initial header timeout is exact JSON HTTP 504 with zero parser/sidecar work; first-body stall is HTTP 200 plus one response.failed/log 504; a later stall occurs after exactly one completed sidecar search; non-2xx read failures preserve status and leak no body error.

criteria.scenario: Parent cancellation and bounded 429 credential rotation remain correct under the new timeout boundaries
criteria.expectedEvidence: Existing and added abort/429 tests pass without hangs
criteria.capturedEvidence: Migrated web-search and sidecar-abort suites pass 35/35, including explicit 499 parent cancellation and cumulative 429 deadline reuse. The runtime contract proves a never-settling outer 429 body cancel cannot block rotation. Google/Kiro retry suites pass 30/30 with never-settling and sync-throwing cancellation cleanup covered and independently re-reviewed PASS.

criteria.scenario: Client-facing errors, request logs, configuration naming, and transport documentation identify the exact phase that timed out
criteria.expectedEvidence: Diff inspection plus wording assertions and source-of-truth consistency audit
criteria.capturedEvidence: Exact header/body timeout strings and 504/upstream_server_error logging pass in runtime, request-log, and WebSocket suites. routedModelStallTimeoutMs defaults/falls back to 200000, validates 1..2147483647, and is threaded into the bridge maximum. Independent SOT plus EN/KO/ZH documentation review and git diff --check both PASS.

criteria.scenario: The affected suites, project typecheck/build gates, and an independent sol-medium code review report no blocking failure
criteria.expectedEvidence: Bun test and typecheck/build output, git diff audit, and normalized reviewer verdict
criteria.capturedEvidence: Affected aggregate passes 271 tests across 21 files with 795 assertions. Full bun test ./tests/ passes 2003 tests across 199 files with 8505 assertions and 0 failures in 28.79s. bun run typecheck and git diff --check exit 0. Final sol-medium review reports VERDICT: PASS with no production, security, cleanup, or LM Studio compatibility blocker; dirty-overlap audit also PASS.
